text:
Cameron Norriewithdrew from the Olympics with an injury hours before he was due to take to the court for his first-round match.

It comes after world No. 6 Alex De Minaur andUkraine's Anhelina Kalinina pulled out before play started on day two of the tennis event at the Paris 2024 Games.

Norrie was set to face Tallon Griekspoor third on Court 10. But when the first match on the court finished, with Marie Lourdes Carle beating Tatjana Maria 6-0 6-0, it was announced that Norrie had forfeited his spot in the Olympics.

The LTA - Britain's governing body for tennis - tweeted: "Sadly Cam Norrie has been forced to withdraw from @TeamGB at the @Olympics due to injury Hope to see you back on court soon, Cam."

The world No. 44 was set to make his Olympic debut but he will now have to wait until LA 2028 if he wants to compete in his first Games. Norrie has been replaced in the draw by Petros Tsitsipas, the younger brother ofStefanos Tsitsipaswho is currently unranked in singles and has a career-high of No. 727.

And he wasn't the only player to pull out on Sunday. In the morning, the Australian Olympic team confirmed that De Minaur had not recovered in time to compete. The world No. 6 also pulled out of his recentWimbledonquarter-final after injuring his hip in his previous match. He will still play in the men's doubles at the Paris Games.

Just in:Carlos Alcaraz makes telling Rafael Nadal admission after Olympics debut

The Australian team wrote: "De Minaur has been rehabbing a hip injury he sustained during the fourth round at Wimbledon. He has tried to do everything possible to get himself ready to play singles at the Olympics. His first-round match was scheduled for later today (Sunday 28 July) against Jan-Lennard Struff of Germany, but Alex will prioritise men’s doubles alongside his childhood friend, Alexei Popyrin."

The International Tennis Federation later confirmed that top 50 star Kalinina pulled out of the women's singles competition. She was replaced by Olivia Gadecki of Australia, while Portugal's Francisco Cabral took De Minaur's spot in the draw.

There has been controversy over the withdrawals in the singles draws at the Olympics, as doubles players are subbed in to take their place - a ruleNovak Djokovicslammed on Saturday when he demolished doubles No. 3 Matthew Ebden, who had not played a singles match since 2022.

Don't miss:Coco Gauff details bathroom drama that saw entire team ditch Olympic village

Don't miss:Andy Murray snubbed for potential retirement match as Olympic call made

He dropped just one game in a 53-minute thrashing andlater hit out at the ITF and IOC."I don’t think it’s a good image for the sport, to be honest. There were a lot of singles players that had plenty of time, that were alternates, that could’ve been called to come," he explained.

"So this part I don’t get, I really hope the ITF along with the Olympics will consider changing this rule because it’s tough on Matthew because, he hasn’t played I guess a singles practice in two years. He told me it’s been over two years [since] he played a singles match, this was his last singles match."

If a player takes themself out of the singles draw after July 19, they can only be replaced by a doubles athlete who has already been called up to represent their nation at the Olympics.

title: Cameron Norrie becomes third tennis star to pull out of Olympics in one day | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Yasmin Syed
description: British tennis star Cameron Norrie became the latest tennis player to pull out of the Olympics.
